# terrible_amazon_aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `appliance_mode_support` (Boolean) Whether the attachment is configured for appliance mode. When appliance mode is enabled, Transit Gateway, using 4-tuples of an IP packet, selects a single Transit Gateway ENI in the Appliance VPC for the life of a flow to send traffic to.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dns_support` (Boolean) Whether DNS support is enabled.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ipv6_support` (Boolean) Whether IPv6 support is enabled.
- `name` (String) The `Name` tag of the Transit Gateway attachment. Providing both `id` and `name` will set the `Name` tag on an existing attachment the matching `id`. Setting the `Name` tag in `tags` will also result in the `Name` tag being updated. At least one of `name`, `transit_gateway` and `id` must be provided.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `purge_subnets` (Boolean) If `purge_subnets=true`, existing subnets will be removed from the attachment as necessary to match exactly what is defined by `subnets`.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create or remove the Transit Gateway attachment.
- `subnets` (String) The ID of the subnets in which to create the transit gateway VPC attachment. Required when creating a new attachment.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `transit_gateway` (String) The ID of the Transit Gateway that the attachment belongs to. When creating a new attachment, `transit_gateway` must be provided. At least one of `name`, `transit_gateway` and `id` must be provided. `transit_gateway` is an immutable setting and can not be updated on an existing attachment.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `wait` (Boolean) Whether to wait for the Transit Gateway attachment to reach the `Available` or `Deleted` state before the module returns.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the Transit Gateway attachment to reach the expected state. Defaults to 600 seconds.

### Read-Only

- `attachments` (String) The attributes of the Transit Gateway attachments.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
